```{r setup, include=TRUE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)


library(nloptr)

# --- 1. DEFINE CONSTANTS ---
N <- 100               # Number of discrete time steps
dt <- 5.0            # Time step duration (seconds) - Total flight time = 500s
g0 <- 9.81            # Sea-level gravity (m/s^2)
Isp <- 311            # Specific impulse of Falcon Heavy Vacuum engine (seconds)
U_max <- 934000       # Max thrust of Merlin Vacuum engine (Newtons)
m_dry <- 22200        # Empty mass of Falcon 9 upper stage + payload (kg)
m_initial <- 111500   # Fully fueled initial mass (kg)
target_alt <- 1000000 # 1000 km target altitude (meters)
target_vel <- 7350    # Orbital velocity at 1000 km (m/s)


# NEW: Staging Constants
N_stage <- 25              # The exact node where staging happens (Splice Point)
m_booster_drop <- 30000    # The mass of the empty boosters falling away (kg)

# NEW: Updated unpack function to extract the Free Time variable
unpack_state <- function(x) {
  list(
    h = x[1:N],
    v = x[(N + 1):(2 * N)],
    m = x[(2 * N + 1):(3 * N)],
    u = x[(3 * N + 1):(4 * N)],
    t_f = x[4 * N + 1]       # The very last number is our Total Flight Time!
  )
}


# --- 2. SETUP GLOBAL TRACKING (Must go here!) ---
iteration_history <<- list()
eval_count <<- 0

# --- 3. THE OBJECTIVE FUNCTION ---
eval_f <- function(x) {
  state <- unpack_state(x)
  eval_count <<- eval_count + 1
  
  # Take a snapshot of the altitude array every 500 evaluations
  if (eval_count %% 500 == 0) {
    iteration_history[[length(iteration_history) + 1]] <<- state$h
  }
  
  return(-state$m[N]) 
}

# --- 4. EQUALITY CONSTRAINTS (Physics) ---
# nloptr expects this function to return a vector of values that must equal ZERO.
eval_g_eq <- function(x) {
  state <- unpack_state(x)
  eq_constraints <- numeric(0)
  
  # NEW: Time step is now dynamically chosen by the solver
  dt <- state$t_f / (N - 1)
  # A. Initial State Constraints (Launch Pad)
  eq_constraints <- c(eq_constraints, state$h[1] - 0)           # Start at altitude 0
  eq_constraints <- c(eq_constraints, state$v[1] - 0)           # Start at velocity 0
  eq_constraints <- c(eq_constraints, state$m[1] - m_initial)   # Start fully fueled
  
  # B. Terminal State Constraints (1000 km Orbit)
  eq_constraints <- c(eq_constraints, state$h[N] - target_alt)  # End at 1000km
  eq_constraints <- c(eq_constraints, state$v[N] - target_vel)  # End at orbital speed
  
  # C. System Dynamics (Euler Integration across all time steps)
  #Physics loop
  for (k in 1:(N - 1)) {
    # Gravity gets weaker with altitude (Newton's law approximation)
    g_current <- g0 * (6371000 / (6371000 + state$h[k]))^2
    
    # Kinematics: h[k+1] = h[k] + v[k]*dt
    dh <- state$h[k+1] - (state$h[k] + state$v[k] * dt)
    
    # Kinetics: v[k+1] = v[k] + (Thrust/Mass - Gravity) * dt
    dv <- state$v[k+1] - (state$v[k] + (state$u[k] / state$m[k] - g_current) * dt)
    
    # NEW: THE SPLICE POINT
    if (k == N_stage) {
      # At this exact step, subtract fuel burned AND the empty booster mass
      dm <- state$m[k+1] - (state$m[k] - m_booster_drop - (state$u[k] / (Isp * g0)) * dt)
    } else {
      # Normal mass depletion
      dm <- state$m[k+1] - (state$m[k] - (state$u[k] / (Isp * g0)) * dt)
    }
    
    eq_constraints <- c(eq_constraints, dh, dv, dm)
  }
  
  return(eq_constraints)
}




# --- 4. INITIAL GUESS & BOUNDS ---
# Guess it takes about 600 seconds to reach orbit
t_f_guess <- 600 

t_guess <- seq(0, 1, length.out = N)
x0_h <- target_alt * (t_guess^2)      
x0_v <- target_vel * t_guess          
x0_m <- seq(m_initial, m_dry, length.out = N)
x0_u <- rep(U_max * 0.8, N) 

# Combine arrays AND append the time guess to the end
x0 <- c(x0_h, x0_v, x0_m, x0_u, t_f_guess)

# Define Bounds (Adding limits for our new Time variable)
lb <- c(rep(0, N), rep(-1000, N), rep(m_dry, N), rep(0, N), 100)  # Time must be > 100s            
ub <- c(rep(target_alt * 2, N), rep(target_vel * 2, N), rep(m_initial, N), rep(U_max, N), 2000) # Time < 2000s        

# --- 5. RUN THE SOLVER ---
opts <- list(
  "algorithm" = "NLOPT_LN_COBYLA", 
  "xtol_rel" = 1.0e-5,          
  "maxeval" = 20000,            
  "print_level" = 1
)

result <- nloptr(x0 = x0, eval_f = eval_f, eval_g_eq = eval_g_eq, lb = lb, ub = ub, opts = opts
)


# Unpack and Print 
optimal_trajectory <- unpack_state(result$solution)

cat("\n--- OPTIMAL FINAL STATE ---\n")
cat("Final Altitude:", optimal_trajectory$h[N], "meters\n")
cat("Final Velocity:", optimal_trajectory$v[N], "m/s\n")
cat("Final Mass:    ", optimal_trajectory$m[N], "kg\n")


#Extract the best mathematical vector the solver found
best_x <- result$solution

#Unpack that giant vector back into physical rocket states
optimal_trajectory <- unpack_state(best_x)

cat(result$message, "\n")
print(result)

# --- 7. ANIMATE THE RESULTS ---
time_steps <- seq(0, (N-1)*dt, by=dt)

# Define our Splice Points (Assuming MECO happens around 150 seconds)
t_MECO <- 150       # Main Engine Cutoff
t_StageSep <- 155   # First Stage separates (Mass drops instantaneously)
t_SES <- 160        # Second Engine Start

# Set up a 3-row, 1-column layout for stacked graphs
par(mfrow = c(3, 1), mar = c(4, 4, 2, 1))

# 1. Altitude Graph
plot(time_steps, optimal_trajectory$h / 1000, type="l", lwd=3, col="darkblue",
     main="Altitude vs Time", xlab="", ylab="Altitude (km)")
# Add Splice Lines
abline(v = c(t_MECO, t_StageSep, t_SES), col=c("red", "black", "green"), lty=2, lwd=2)
legend("topleft", legend=c("MECO", "Stage Sep", "SES"), col=c("red", "black", "green"), lty=2)

# 2. Velocity Graph (You will see the acceleration change here!)
plot(time_steps, optimal_trajectory$v, type="l", lwd=3, col="darkorange",
     main="Velocity vs Time", xlab="", ylab="Velocity (m/s)")
abline(v = c(t_MECO, t_StageSep, t_SES), col=c("red", "black", "green"), lty=2, lwd=2)

# 3. Mass Depletion Graph (Showing the instantaneous mass drop)
plot(time_steps, optimal_trajectory$m / 1000, type="l", lwd=3, col="purple",
     main="Vehicle Mass vs Time", xlab="Time (seconds)", ylab="Mass (Metric Tons)")
abline(v = c(t_MECO, t_StageSep, t_SES), col=c("red", "black", "green"), lty=2, lwd=2)

# Reset plot layout to default
par(mfrow = c(1, 1))




##############################$%&*&^%^&**$&^*^%$*&^&*&^^&

# --- 7. ANIMATE THE RESULTS ---
time_steps <- seq(0, (N-1)*dt, by=dt)

cat("Playing animation in Plot viewer...\n")
for(i in 1:length(iteration_history)) {
  plot(time_steps, iteration_history[[i]] / 1000, type="l", 
       lwd=2, col="gray",
       ylim=c(0, (target_alt * 1.2) / 1000),
       main=paste("Optimizer Iteration:", i * 500),
       xlab="Time (seconds)", 
       ylab="Altitude (km)")
  
  Sys.sleep(0.1) 
}

# Draw the final optimal solution clearly over the top
lines(time_steps, optimal_trajectory$h / 1000, lwd=3, col="blue")

```
